title: Why Artemis II Isn't Landing on the Moon
summary: NASA is sending a big rocket called Artemis II to the Moon with astronauts for the first time in over 50 years. But this mission won't land on the Moon. Instead, it's a practice trip to get ready for a future landing. The goal is to help astronauts learn how to travel to the Moon safely and explore space further. The rocket is launching from Florida, and people are watching it live. This mission is part of a bigger plan to send humans back to the Moon and maybe even to Mars one day. It's exciting because it helps us learn more about space and prepares for bigger adventures.

questions.correct_answer: To send humans back to the Moon and explore space further

background_read: Space exploration is when humans send rockets and astronauts to learn about space. NASA is a group in the U.S. that does this. The Moon is Earth's closest neighbor in space, and people first landed there in 1969. The Artemis program is NASA's new plan to go back to the Moon. Artemis II is a mission where astronauts will fly around the Moon without landing, like a test drive, to make sure everything works before trying to land later. This helps keep astronauts safe and prepares for future trips to explore more of space, like going to Mars.
Article_Structure: WHAT: Sending Artemis II to the Moon without landing
WHY: To practice for a future lunar landing and explore space further

perspectives.description: This mission helps humans learn more about space and prepare for future adventures.
